steps.title: Identify the stated reason for changing approaches
steps.content: The passage explains that Walsh used intact microbial communities from natural settings to better reflect the variation and complexity of naturally occurring communities. This reveals the limitation of Baslam's method: adding only specific strains.

steps.title: Match the methodological limitation to the correct choice
steps.content:
Adding only specific bacterial or fungal strains creates a microbial composition that won't match naturally occurring ones—what choice A describes.

Choices B, C, and D either contradict the passage (B claims the spinach study didn't affect flavor, though it clearly increased flavonoid content; C reverses the comparison by claiming spinach's composition was similar to wild soils; D invents unsupported claims about abnormal baseline diversity).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
